text.title: Single Page Case Study Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules so every finished freelance engagement produces a reusable, evidence-backed, NDA-safe sales asset within one week.

rule.statement: The case study MUST fill all six required slots — problem, approach, outcome, numbers, pull-quote, NDA-fallback indicator — and fits on one page (≤500 words plus one chart).
rule.rationale: Freelancers never write case studies because there is no template; a six-slot fixed structure removes the blank-page problem. The one-page constraint forces ruthless editing and matches how prospects actually consume case studies (scan, not read).

example.code:
# How <Client> cut checkout latency 62% in 3 weeks
**Problem:** ...one sentence... **Approach:** ...two-to-three sentences with stack... **Outcome:** ...one sentence with numbers... **Numbers:** - p95 checkout: 1480 ms → 560 ms (-62%) - Stripe declined-card retries: 18% → 6% - Implementation cost: 3 weeks, fixed-scope $X
**Quote:** "..." — <Name>, <Role>, <Company>
NDA: full version available on request.

rule.statement: The "numbers" slot MUST contain at least one quantitative outcome with a baseline, a final value, and a relative delta; vague "significant improvement" or single-number-without-baseline entries are rejected.
rule.rationale: Service-engagement case studies live or die on credible numbers. SaaS-feature-launch templates fail freelance work because they assume product analytics; freelancers must explicitly attach before/after to a defensible source (Stripe, server logs, calendar, P&L).

rule.statement: The pull-quote MUST be a verbatim sentence from the named client (role + company), captured in writing with explicit publish permission; paraphrased "the client was very happy" is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Prospects discount marketer-paraphrased praise to zero; named-human quotes carry the trust. Capturing in writing also protects the freelancer if a client later disputes attribution.

example.description: "The team loved the new system" — no name, no role, no company.
example.reason: Indistinguishable from invented copy; prospects mentally discount it to zero.

rule.statement: When the client refuses naming, the methodology MUST produce an NDA-safe anonymous version with industry + company-size + role-of-quoted-person disclosed instead; the case study is never silently dropped.
rule.rationale: Confidential engagements are the freelancer's most lucrative work but currently never become sales assets because there is no fallback path. The structured-anonymous version preserves 70% of the credibility (sector + scale + numbers) while honouring the NDA.

example.code: **Client (anonymised):** Series-B fintech, 80 engineers, EU-regulated. **Quote:** "..." — VP Engineering (named in private reference list).

rule.statement: The case study MUST be drafted within 7 days of final delivery and published / parked within 14; engagements older than 30 days without a case study are dead-letter and the methodology is not applied retroactively.
rule.rationale: Memory decay on engagement detail is steep — pull-quotes become impossible to capture once the client has moved on. A one-week draft deadline aligned with delivery is the load-bearing constraint that makes every engagement compound into a marketing asset.
